title: Sensitive Data Transmitted in URL
description: Placing sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII) in URL query parameters exposes it in server logs, browser history, and HTTP Referer headers (OWASP A02:2021 / CWE-598).

finding.message: Sensitive data in URL parameter '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Pass sensitive data in the request body (POST/PUT) or in encrypted headers, never in URL query strings.
Ensure server logs are configured to mask or exclude sensitive query parameters.
Use short-lived, opaque tokens in URLs when redirect flows require passing state.
OWASP Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet.
